rules.name: audit_and_observability
rules.overview: Compliance use cases need an immutable audit trail of every guardrail decision. This file covers the AuditLogger utility, the JSONL schema, sink injection, and what to put on dashboards.

mode.name: file
mode.semantics: Append-only JSONL file. Auto-flushes after each record. Parent dirs created on open.

mode.name: sink
mode.semantics: Pluggable callable receives each record dict. Use for streaming to Splunk/ELK/CloudWatch/etc.

mode.name: hash_only
mode.semantics: For regulated industries (healthcare/finance) that forbid persisting user content. The per-record `input` field is dropped; the stable `input_hash` is kept so records remain joinable to source data via a separate (regulated) store.

mode.name: context_manager
mode.semantics: Closes the file on exit. Recommended for scripts.

recording.one_call_per: request, choke point. Don't batch multiple bundles into one record — each stage gets its own line for filterability.

jsonl_schema.description: One JSON object per line, written via `json.dumps(record) + "\n"`.

fields.field: Stable SHA-256 prefix of the input payload. Joinable across records.
Raw input payload. Omitted when include_inputs=False.

splunk_pattern.send: from splunklib.client import connect service = connect(host="...", token="...") audit = AuditLogger(sink=lambda rec: service.indexes["guardrails"].submit( json.dumps(rec), source="real-time-guardrails", sourcetype="_json" ))
splunk_pattern.query: index=guardrails sourcetype=_json overall_action=Block | stats count by blocked_metrics{} request_id

elk_pattern.send: from elasticsearch import Elasticsearch es = Elasticsearch("https://...") audit = AuditLogger(sink=lambda rec: es.index( index="guardrails", document=rec ))

jq_queries.description: useful one-liners against a local audit.jsonl

query.name: block_rate_per_metric
query.code: jq -r '.metrics | to_entries[] | select(.value.action=="Block") | .key' audit.jsonl | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

query.name: latency_per_choke_point
query.code: # requires custom 'choke_point' field added by your wrapper jq -r '.choke_point' audit.jsonl | sort | uniq -c

dashboard.name: block_rate_over_time
dashboard.axes: x: time (1-min buckets); y: block count
dashboard.alert: spike alert if block rate > baseline * 3 for 5 min

dashboard.name: latency_per_choke_point
dashboard.axes: x: time; y: p50, p95, p99
dashboard.alert: p95 > 2s sustained → investigate LLM-judge cost — RULE 12

dashboard.name: llm_judge_token_spend
dashboard.use: track watsonx.ai cost from LLM-as-judge metrics
dashboard.when: only relevant if WXG_PROJECT_ID is set

dashboard.name: per_partner_distribution
dashboard.use: detect a single partner's traffic dragging up overall block rate

latency_budget.metric_category: Granite Guardian (most Safety, RAG-generation, Topic, ToolCallAccuracy)
LLM-as-judge (Answer Completeness, Conciseness, Tool Call Relevance)
latency_budget.rule: Order cheap-first per choke point. Don't run LLM-judge metrics on every request — sample (10% of traffic is a common default).

dashboard_starter.description: How to build a partner-facing dashboard on top of the AuditLogger JSONL. A complete MUI-based reference component is in .bob/reference-payloads/dashboard_skeleton.jsx; the snippets below are the building blocks Bob can use to guide partners through rolling their own.

data_source.recommended: Backend endpoint that tails the JSONL file (see backend_guardrails_proxy.py for a reference: GET /api/audit/recent). Frontend polls every 30 s.
data_source.avoid: Direct browser → JSONL file access. Browser → guardrails REST directly. localStorage as data source (compliance issue — see RULE 15).

visualizations.viz: Pass / Flag / Block counts as one stacked horizontal bar at the top of the dashboard. Counts + percentages labeled inside the segments.
One row per metric: MUI LinearProgress bar showing average score, with text annotation for block/flag/pass counts.
One row per decision, newest first. Expand to see per-metric breakdown (score, action, threshold, flag_threshold).
Optional: per-hour block rate over the last 24h. Out of scope for the basic reference; add when partners need it.

refresh_strategy.approach: 30 s interval. Simple, works through any proxy / firewall. Sufficient for compliance dashboards (not user-facing real-time).
For sub-second latency: convert /api/audit/recent into an SSE stream from the backend. Requires changes to the proxy.
For bidirectional or very high-frequency updates. Heavier lift; rarely justified for a compliance dashboard.

compliance_notes.retention: Default file mode appends forever. For GDPR/CCPA right-to-delete: rotate the JSONL daily and run a periodic redaction job that purges records older than the retention policy.
compliance_notes.pii_in_logs: If you don't use include_inputs=False, raw input may include PII. Either: (a) treat audit logs as PII-class storage with encryption at rest, OR (b) use include_inputs=False and store the original input in a separately-controlled system, joined via input_hash.
compliance_notes.audit_log_chain_of_custody: For regulated industries, sign the JSONL records (e.g. HMAC with a tamper-evident key) before persisting. Out of scope for the SDK but easy to add via the `sink=` callable.
